outcome_contract.why: Work can be marked complete even if it's missing key sections like engineering coverage evidence or outcome statements. Without checking completeness automatically, gaps only surface when someone manually reviews.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Validator pass/fail results are captured in workflow telemetry so completeness gaps are visible in reports without manual review

engineering_coverage.applicable: Pass/fail + error/warning counts visible in reports; sufficient for trend analysis
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Full error messages visible; richer debugging but harder to aggregate
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: aggregation wins; debugging detail is available via the artifact itself

engineering_coverage.applicable: Simple test assertions on fixed shape
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Complex assertions on variable shapes per validator
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option A: uniform shape simplifies test contracts

engineering_coverage.applicable: One typed interface addition; report generator consumes a single shape
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `Record<string, unknown>` requires per-validator type guards or ignoring detail

stage_payload.decision_summary: The fact-find confirmed that deterministic validators produce structured JSON results (pass/fail, error counts, blocking issues) but these results are discarded after the agent reads them. The telemetry record stores only script names. The decision is: what shape should the captured validator data take in the telemetry record, and how should it flow from validators to the record?
goals: Capture validator pass/fail results in telemetry records
Enable the report generator to surface quality/completeness data
non_goals: Changing validator logic or pass/fail criteria
constraints: Backward-compatible with existing records (new field must be optional)
Must not break existing consumers (`summarizeWorkflowStepTelemetry`, `computePerModuleBreakdown`, ideas rollup)
assumptions: All three validators (`validate-fact-find`, `validate-engineering-coverage`, `validate-plan`) produce JSON to stdout
`parseArgs` in the telemetry CLI can handle the chosen input method

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Telemetry JSONL is append-only and grows indefinitely; each record should stay small
Report must show pass/fail and error counts to be useful
Different validators return different types; the captured field should normalize this
Agents pass data via shell flags; complex JSON is error-prone in shell
Report generator should aggregate without knowing validator-specific schemas

options_considered.Description: Add `deterministic_check_results?: Record<string, { valid: boolean; error_count: number; warning_count: number }>` — compact summary keyed by check name
options_considered.Upside: Small record size; uniform shape; easy to aggregate in reports; simple CLI flag design (`--check-result name:valid:errors:warnings`)
options_considered.Downside: Loses detail (specific error messages, task IDs, critique score); can't reconstruct blocking issues from summary alone
options_considered.Key risks: Summary may prove insufficient for debugging; adding more fields later requires another schema bump

options_considered.Description: Add `deterministic_check_results?: Record<string, unknown>` — store entire validator JSON output per check
options_considered.Upside: Full detail preserved; no information loss; easy to pass (just pipe stdout)
options_considered.Downside: Large record size (validator results can be 10+ lines of JSON each); non-uniform shape makes aggregation harder; report generator must know each validator's schema or ignore detail
options_considered.Key risks: Record bloat; consumer complexity; schema evolution couples telemetry to validator internals

options_considered.Description: Write a separate `quality-metrics.jsonl` alongside `telemetry.jsonl` — full detail in a separate stream
options_considered.Upside: No record bloat in main telemetry; full detail preserved; independent evolution
options_considered.Downside: Two files to maintain; report generator must join across streams; breaks single-stream contract; more complex persistence
options_considered.Key risks: Maintenance overhead; join complexity; fragmented observability

chosen_approach.Why this wins: Record size stays small (critical for append-only JSONL that grows indefinitely). Uniform shape (`{ valid, error_count, warning_count }`) means the report generator aggregates without knowing validator internals. CLI flag design is straightforward (`--check-result <name>:<pass|fail>:<errors>:<warnings>`). Tests are simple assertions on a fixed interface. Detail is not lost — it lives in the artifact itself and can be recovered by re-running the validator.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: The compact format must be sufficient for report consumers. If debugging needs arise that require error messages in telemetry, a future schema bump can add an optional `details` field.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (Full results)** — Rejected because it couples telemetry schema evolution to validator internal schemas, bloats records 10-20x, and makes report aggregation harder. The detail it preserves is already available by re-running validators on the artifact.
**Option C (Separate quality log)** — Rejected because it breaks the single-stream contract, adds join complexity, and fragments observability for no clear benefit.

planning_focus: Add `deterministic_check_results` field to `WorkflowStepTelemetryRecord`, builder, and dedupe key
Add `--check-result` CLI flag parsing and record population
Add validator results aggregation to report generator (summary + markdown + JSON)
sequencing_constraints: TASK-01 (interface + builder) must precede TASK-02 (CLI) and TASK-03 (report)
TASK-04 (schema doc) can run in parallel with TASK-03
risks_to_carry_forward: CLI flag parsing for colon-delimited structured data — test with edge cases (validator names containing colons)

end_state_process_areas.Current state: Shell scripts run validators; JSON to stdout; agent reads result
end_state_process_areas.Trigger: Agent completes DO stage, validators pass
end_state_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step end state: Same — no change to validator execution
end_state_process_areas.What remains unchanged: Validator scripts, return types, exit codes

end_state_process_areas.Trigger: Agent calls CLI after validators pass
end_state_process_areas.What remains unchanged: fail>:<errors>:<warnings>` for each validator run. CLI parses these into `deterministic_check_results` field. Record appended with both names and results.
end_state_process_areas.Risks / seams to carry into planning: JSONL path, dedupe logic, all other record fields

end_state_process_areas.Delivered step-by-step end state: Report includes new `## Validator Results` section showing per-check pass/fail rates and aggregate error counts across records. JSON output includes `validator_summary` in envelope.
end_state_process_areas.Risks / seams to carry into planning: Report must handle mixed records (some with results, some without)
